_comment: Bifrost config.json template for the control plane deployment.
Place in Bifrost's app data directory (/app/data/config.json) or configure via Bifrost Web UI.
The canonical schema is bifrost/transports/config.schema.json (~2700 lines).
Secrets use env.VAR_NAME syntax to reference environment variables from bifrost.env.
All fields verified against Bifrost source code in Phase 1 capability audit.
See docs/capability-audit.md for struct definitions and JSON field names.
_clewdr_integration_notes: ClewdR instances expose OpenAI-compatible endpoints at /v1/chat/completions.
To register ClewdR as providers in Bifrost, you have two options:
OPTION A — Custom providers via Bifrost Web UI (recommended):
1. Open Bifrost Web UI (port-forward if needed)
2. Go to Providers -> Add Custom Provider
- Base URL: http://clewdr-1:8484 (Docker DNS resolves this)
- API Key: the CLEWDR_PASSWORD from that instance's env
OPTION B — Routing rules with CEL expressions (advanced):
Use governance.routing_rules to direct specific models to ClewdR.
See the routing_rules section below for an example.
Each ClewdR instance uses a different base_url because each has its
own cookie/account. Bifrost sees them as separate custom providers
and can round-robin or weight-distribute across them.

routing_rules._comment: Example routing rule for lab traffic to ClewdR.
routing_rules._note: CEL expressions verified against Bifrost governance.go. See capability-audit.md.
routing_rules.name: Route lab models to ClewdR pool

auth_config._note: When false, any request on the internal core-net is accepted without auth. Set to true for defense-in-depth. New-API channels would then need valid credentials.
